old_claim.note: Masonry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because levers and rollers are scoped as generic simple-machine transport concepts, while masonry is an application domain rather than a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: UNESCO decision describing Gobekli Tepe monumental stone architecture in the 10th-9th millennia BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors early monumental stone construction, but it does not make masonry a prerequisite for the separate simple-machine idea of levers and rollers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge reversed the application relationship: simple machines can be used in masonry projects, but masonry is not needed to understand or use leverage and rolling transport.

ontology_before: Levers and rollers inherited a later masonry dependency from a generic construction context edge.
ontology_after: Levers and rollers keep woodworking as an earlier material/tool context and no longer depend on masonry.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-ancient-materials-scope keeps this removed edge absent and locks the corrected early-material chronology.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to a later application that truly requires the removed prerequisite.
A stronger source showed the removed prerequisite existed earlier than and was necessary for the dependent node scope.
A future graph split introduced a narrower downstream node where this removed edge would be correctly scoped.
short_reason: No direct dependency remains because levers and rollers are scoped as generic simple-machine transport concepts, while masonry is an application domain rather than a prerequisite.
